summary: The live SQLite skill-graph path uses WAL mode and wraps index mutation in one database transaction, so concurrent readers should observe a committed old-or-new snapshot rather than half-written rows.

The fallback compiler overwrites skill-graph.json directly with open-write semantics, and the advisor can consume that JSON when SQLite is unavailable, so the non-SQLite path still has a truncate/rewrite race unless it is upgraded to temp-file-plus-rename.

Once _SKILL_GRAPH is populated, the advisor reuses it without a freshness probe, while SKILL discovery still refreshes from a path+mtime_ns+size signature, allowing concurrent sessions to diverge on graph boosts even when live disk state changed.

Claude hook state writes use unique temp files plus rename and readers reject mtime_changed candidates, but updateState still performs an unlocked load-merge-save cycle, so concurrent writers to the same session can lose disjoint field updates.

The cached session summary helpers can search by specFolder scope and then choose the newest matching state, which means session continuity selection is only strictly session-local when claudeSessionId is carried through to the lookup.

Skill-graph rebuilds should acquire a workspace-scoped advisory lock and publish a generation-tagged snapshot identity so readers can either reuse the last committed snapshot or reload after commit without trusting stale process memory.

The JSON fallback path should either switch to temp-file-plus-rename publication or be treated as unavailable during rebuilds; parsing the in-place rewrite target during concurrent export is not a safe consistency model.

Atomic rename is insufficient for semantic correctness on read-merge-write updates; hook-state needs either a per-session advisory lock or a revisioned compare-and-swap retry loop so concurrent patches converge instead of clobbering.

Coarse TTLs and eventual process restarts were ruled out as the primary fix because they reduce staleness windows but do not provide committed snapshot guarantees, stale-cache detection, or lost-update protection for same-session hook-state writes.
